title: Craft Recipes from Harley MS 2253, f. 52v
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan item CME00137, a manually keyed and proofed electronic text of Thomas Wright's 1844 article 'Early English Receipts for Painting, Gilding, &c.' Wright transcribed the added Middle English craft directions on British Library Harley MS 2253, f.52v, supplied an introduction and fourteen lettered notes, and printed the article on pp.64-66 of Archaeological Journal volume 1. The serial issue is dated 1844, while the bound-volume title page is dated 1845. The XML contains nine recipe paragraphs; current British Library and METS descriptions number eight manuscript articles because Wright's separate gold-foil paragraph is a continuation within METS art.15. The f.52v addition is excluded from the main Harley scribe's c.1330-40 copying: current scholarship assigns it to the later hand called Scribe C, probably not much later than the main hand. Wright's in-or-soon-after-1307 estimate and the MED's ?a1350 citation remain unresolved competing claims. A complete 482-page Internet Archive scan and complete OCR/scandata/metadata derivatives are cached, as is the complete 1,448-page METS three-volume edition. British Library catalogs full manuscript image coverage but currently marks the images unavailable; the 1965 Ker facsimile and 2016 Clarke edition remain findable through library access routes.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual subject is the anonymous Middle English collection or sequence on f.52v. The physical witness, Wright article, journal carrier, U-M encoding, repository XML, modern editions, scans, catalogs, and scholarship remain distinct entities.
Eight is the manuscript-article count in the current BL/METS inventory; nine is the Wright/U-M paragraph count. The graph preserves both without creating nine independently authored medieval works.
The two XML L elements are Chaucer lines quoted inside Wright's footnote i. They belong to edition paratext and do not make the medieval work verse or mixed-form.

The British Library component record explicitly excludes f.52v from the Harley scribe's work. Its c.1330-40 date, West Midlands/Ludlow context, and whole-part language profile are therefore not transferred mechanically to Scribe C or the recipes.
Wright's 1844 article date and the 1845 title-page date of the completed bound volume describe different bibliographic layers and are preserved separately.
The Internet Archive PDF is complete relative to the 482-page provider scan and full bound volume. Its target is PDF pp.82-84, scan leaves n84-n86, printed pp.64-66; it is not a crop of the article.

The METS PDF is complete relative to all three edition volumes. qpdf succeeds with linearization hint-table warnings; pages render and text extracts, so the warning category is disclosed rather than treated as missing content.
The Sobecki cache file is the complete provider-published 50-page open introduction but partial relative to the 2020 book. It is never described as a complete book download.

Public access is not a reuse license. Rights are separated for the medieval text, Wright edition, U-M encoding, repository XML, IA derivatives, BL catalog metadata, manuscript images, Ker facsimile, METS edition, Sobecki chapter, and Clarke edition.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00137
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

date_statements.label: pinned repository commit
date_statements.notes: Commit date for the pinned CME source snapshot, not creation of the U-M encoding or publication of the work.

notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00137; the file is 10,231 bytes.
The exact pinned raw GitHub object was fetched live on 2026-07-13 and matched the checked-in file byte-for-byte and by SHA-256.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00137
entities.description: A public electronic text of Wright's complete three-page article, produced within the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse by manual keying from a public-domain edition with in-house proofreading and editing. It preserves one modern introduction, nine recipe paragraphs, fourteen notes, three printed-page milestones, and Wright's signature.

date_statements.label: recorded proofing and structural review
date_statements.notes: TEMPHEAD change date for U-M/repository review; not necessarily the initial electronic publication date.

entities.reproduction_format: Basic TEI-like XML electronic text produced by manual keying and proofreading
notes: TEXT/@LANG='enm eng' describes medieval and editorial layers together. Latin and the Chaucer quotation occur only in Wright's notes.

entities.label: Craft Recipes from Harley MS 2253, f. 52v
alternate_labels: Early English Receipts for Painting, Gilding, &c.
entities.description: An anonymous Middle English prose collection or manuscript-specific sequence of practical directions for pigments, metal-leaf application, iron hardening, and white-lead manufacture. Wright and the U-M XML divide it into nine procedural paragraphs; BL and METS describe eight manuscript articles because the gold-foil continuation belongs to the silver-foil article.
notes: Composition date, source exemplars, authorship, and original organization remain unknown.
This entity describes the intellectual content, not Wright's article as a bibliographic object and not Harley MS 2253 as a physical codex.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2253, f. 52v recipe addition
entities.description: The unique known manuscript witness of the target collection, written by the later hand conventionally called Scribe C in a column and a half of blank space on f.52v at the end of Booklet 3. The current BL component record labels it '8 Recipes in English (added)' and explicitly excludes f.52v from the main Harley scribe's work.

date_statements.label: Wright's historical estimate
date_statements.notes: Wright's 1844 claim based on associating the manuscript text with the beginning of Edward II's reign; preserved as attributed evidence, not current consensus.

date_statements.label: Middle English Dictionary source date
date_statements.notes: Current uncertain MED date for Recipe Painting (1), kept distinct from Wright's estimate.

date_statements.label: current relative codicological chronology
date_statements.value: later Scribe C addition, probably not much later than the c.1330-40 main hand
date_statements.notes: No exact normalized year range is imposed. The BL aggregate c.1330-40 component date cannot automatically date the explicitly excluded f.52v.

holding.notes: Target on f.52v; request the manuscript by parent shelfmark and consult all manuscripts in London.

physical_description.extent: f.52v; approximately one and a half written columns within a codex of ff.1* + 142 and modern paper flyleaves
physical_description.format: Added English prose recipes in Gothic cursive script
physical_description.dimensions: Whole codex 280-290 x 190 mm; written area 215 x 145 mm
notes: Whole-codex dimensions, support, foliation, and 1963 rebinding come from the parent record.
The parent record's West Midlands/Ludlow origin and the component record's c.1330-40 main-scribe date do not establish where or when Scribe C copied the target.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, Early English Receipts for Painting, Gilding, &c. (1844)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of the U-M encoding. Wright supplies a modern English introduction, transcribes nine Middle English recipe paragraphs from Harley MS 2253 f.52v, adds fourteen lettered explanatory and textual notes, and signs the article T. WRIGHT.

date_statements.label: serial publication
date_statements.notes: Article/issue year. The completed volume's title page is dated 1845.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, 'Early English Receipts for Painting, Gilding, &c.,' Archaeological Journal 1 (1844): 64-66.
bibliographic.title: Early English Receipts for Painting, Gilding, &c.

notes: Wright's introductory dating claim is edition history, not silently accepted as the recipe collection's composition date.
The Chaucer quotation in note i is Wright's editorial comparison and is not manuscript recipe content.

entities.label: Archaeological Journal, volume 1
entities.description: The complete serial volume carrying Wright's article. The journal and ADS issue record date volume 1 to 1844, while the bound-volume title page is dated MDCCCXLV (1845); both statements are retained at this carrier layer.

date_statements.label: bound-volume title-page date
date_statements.notes: Carrier title-page date, not a correction to the article's 1844 serial year.

bibliographic.citation: The Archaeological Journal, vol. 1 (London: Longman, Brown, Green, and Longmans, 1845; serial year 1844).
bibliographic.title: The Archaeological Journal

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Archaeological Journal volume 1
entities.description: A complete 482-page downloadable scan of the bound volume, including covers, preliminaries, plates, continuously numbered journal pages through p.419, index, membership list, blanks, and back matter. Wright's article is PDF pp.82-84, scan leaves n84-n86, printed pp.64-66.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 482-page PDF with full OCR and scandata derivatives
notes: The provider PDF is complete relative to the bound source volume; it was not cropped to the target article.
Scandata enumerates 490 leaves: 482 addToAccessFormats=true leaves represented in the PDF and eight excluded deletion/calibration frames.

entities.label: Internet Archive metadata for archaeologicaljo01brit
entities.description: Provider metadata for the complete Archaeological Journal volume 1 scan and its derivatives, including identifier, title, volume, date, contributor, scanner, PPI, sizes, and fixity fields. No explicit rights or license field is supplied.

entities.label: British Library digital facsimile of Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The parent catalog states that full digital coverage exists, but the same current record marks the digital images unavailable during service recovery. No manuscript image file was cached.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete manuscript coverage cataloged; current image delivery unavailable

entities.label: Facsimile of British Museum MS Harley 2253 (Ker, 1965)
entities.description: Neil R. Ker's EETS photographic facsimile with introduction. It reproduces the latter manuscript portion beginning at f.49 and includes target f.52v, but excludes the first forty-eight leaves and is not a complete facsimile of the codex.

bibliographic.citation: Facsimile of British Museum MS Harley 2253, intro. Neil R. Ker, Early English Text Society, Original Series 255 (London: British Museum, 1965).
bibliographic.title: Facsimile of British Museum MS Harley 2253
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 255

entities.reproduction_format: Printed photographic facsimile with scholarly introduction; partial codex coverage, complete target coverage
notes: No unrestricted public digital copy was verified. The British Library holds a requestable physical surrogate as MS Facsimile 764.

entities.label: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, volumes 1-3
entities.description: Susanna Fein's complete-manuscript scholarly edition, with Anglo-Norman translations by David Raybin and Latin translations by Jan Ziolkowski. It edits and translates the f.52v target as articles 10-17, treating the silver- and gold-foil directions as one article 15.

bibliographic.citation: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, ed. Susanna Fein, with David Raybin and Jan Ziolkowski, 3 vols., Middle English Texts Series (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2014-2015).
bibliographic.title: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript

entities.reproduction_format: Complete three-volume scholarly edition and facing translation, 1,448-page downloadable PDF plus web text
notes: The complete cached PDF places the target at combined PDF pp.556-561, corresponding to volume 2 printed pp.46-51; the Booklet 3 boundary discussion is combined PDF p.516 / volume 2 p.6.

entities.label: Sebastian Sobecki, Harley Manuscript Geographies (2020)
entities.description: A modern monograph on the manuscript's literary, codicological, and geographical organization. The open introduction identifies Scribe C's use of blank f.52v for the craft recipes and gives a relative chronology of probably not much later than the main hand.

bibliographic.citation: Sebastian Sobecki, Harley Manuscript Geographies: Literary History and the Medieval Miscellany (Manchester: Manchester University Press, 2020).
bibliographic.title: Harley Manuscript Geographies: Literary History and the Medieval Miscellany

notes: The cached source is the complete open introduction chapter only, not the complete monograph.

entities.label: Mark Clarke, The Crafte of Lymmyng and the Maner of Steynyng (2016)
entities.description: A modern EETS edition of Middle English recipes for painters, stainers, scribes, and illuminators. Current MED quotations cite the Harley recipe text to Clarke pp.229-230; the book was identified through an official Yale catalog, but the target pages were not downloaded or directly inspected.

bibliographic.citation: The Crafte of Lymmyng and the Maner of Steynyng: Middle English Recipes for Painters, Stainers, Scribes, and Illuminators, ed. Mark Clarke, Early English Text Society, Original Series 347 (Oxford: Oxford University Press for EETS, 2016).
bibliographic.title: The Crafte of Lymmyng and the Maner of Steynyng: Middle English Recipes for Painters, Stainers, Scribes, and Illuminators
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 347
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: Modeled as a later scholarly edition and comparison source, not an immediate source of Wright or U-M.
No restricted or unauthorized copy was cached.

entities.description: The current MED bibliography/source citation '?a1350 Recipe Painting(1) in Archaeol.J.1 (Hrl 2253) 64-66,' with updated quotations in some entries also citing Clarke's Lymmyng pp.229-230.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Added English craft directions occupying f.52v
editorial_practices.representation: Nine transcribed recipe paragraphs with a modern introduction and fourteen explanatory footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Wright selects and publishes the target f.52v recipe addition rather than editing Harley MS 2253 as a whole.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete recipe addition as understood by Wright; surrounding manuscript contents excluded
notes: No explicit comprehensive transcription policy was found in the three-page article, so abbreviation, punctuation, and capitalization behavior are not generalized beyond visible evidence.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Wright's article on printed pp.64-66 within a complete journal volume
editorial_practices.representation: One encoded DIV1 with introduction and recipes DIV2 children, retaining notes and page breaks
editorial_practices.description: U-M manually keys Wright's target article rather than the complete Archaeological Journal volume.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete article, including Wright's paratext and medieval transcription
notes: The XML's nine recipe P elements follow Wright's printed paragraphing. They do not override the eight-article manuscript inventory.

rights.basis: The sole witness is a medieval addition made centuries before modern copyright terms; this statement concerns the historical text only, not editions, transcriptions, metadata, or photographs.

rights.component: Wright's 1844 article text, transcription, introduction, and notes
rights.basis: Wright's article was first published in 1844 and is in the public domain in the United States by age. This claim concerns the historical edition and its contents, not U-M's later electronic encoding or interface.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and displayed encoding
rights.basis: U-M labels the item Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, but the public page does not separately define the scope of that statement for markup, data, and interface elements.
notes: This records the provider's item-level statement, not an independent conclusion that every encoding contribution is uncopyrightable.

rights.basis: The CME root LICENSE contains Unlicense language but describes 'software'; no path-specific statement confirms its intended application to XML markup or data.
notes: Do not treat the root file as a grant over Wright's edition, U-M's encoding, manuscript photography, or modern scholarship.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, and scandata derivative files
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive metadata supplies no explicit rights status or reuse license for the digital derivative files. Public download availability does not itself establish reuse permission.
notes: Underlying Wright content has a separate No Copyright-United States provider statement at U-M.

rights.component: British Library catalog text and JSON metadata
rights.basis: The current catalog footer attributes catalog content to the British Library Board and states that it is made available under a CC-BY licence; the page does not expose a version number in the captured record.

license.name: CC BY (version not specified by catalog page)

notes: This license statement applies to catalog content, not automatically to manuscript images or third-party text quoted in the record.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photography and image delivery
rights.basis: The catalog's CC-BY footer is not treated as an image license, the images are currently unavailable, and no target-image reuse statement could be inspected.

rights.component: 1965 photographic facsimile, layout, and Ker introduction
rights.basis: The facsimile was published in 1965 and no open reuse license was found in the public catalog records. Medieval-text public-domain status does not determine rights in modern photography or introduction.

rights.component: METS scholarly texts, translations, introductions, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: The scholarly edition and translations were published in 2014-2015. METS describes its collection as Open Access and supplies free downloads, but no explicit reuse license was found on the inspected pages or cached PDF.
notes: Open access is recorded as access, not converted into a public-domain or Creative Commons claim.

rights.basis: The chapter is a contemporary scholarly publication. The provider offers the introduction openly, but no explicit reuse license was found in the file.

rights.component: 2016 scholarly edition, introduction, apparatus, and facsimile plate
rights.basis: The edition is a contemporary 2016 publication; the Yale holding record says Copyright Not Evaluated and supplies no open license.

rights.component: MED definitions, bibliography, editorial data, and web presentation
rights.basis: The historical quotations are separable from modern dictionary definitions, editorial bibliography, database structure, and interface. No broad reuse license is inferred from public access.

open_questions.question: What component-level evidence can resolve Wright's in-or-soon-after-1307 date, the MED's ?a1350 citation, and Scribe C's relative chronology?
next_steps: Inspect Ker's introduction and Clarke's discussion of the Harley recipes.
Consult current paleographical work specifically on Scribe C and f.52v.
Do not normalize the aggregate c.1330-40 Part II date onto f.52v unless a source explicitly includes the added hand.

open_questions.question: Who was Scribe C, and can the hand or recipe text be localized independently of the main Ludlow scribe?
next_steps: Trace Scribe C hand identifications and linguistic analyses in manuscript scholarship.
Seek item-level LALME or eLALME evidence rather than borrowing the whole-codex Ludlow attribution.
Keep work composition place, witness copying place, and main-scribe activity distinct.

open_questions.question: What source traditions and item boundaries underlie the eight manuscript articles and nine Wright/U-M procedural paragraphs?
next_steps: Collate f.52v, Wright, METS, and Clarke against recipe indexes and analogues.
Determine whether the gold-foil continuation has independent transmission evidence or should remain subordinate to art.15.
Avoid treating each XML P as an independently authored work without textual evidence.

open_questions.question: When will a current first-party image of f.52v be available for direct layout and scribal verification?
next_steps: Recheck the British Library digital service after recovery.
Request the physical manuscript in London if necessary.
Consult the requestable Ker facsimile while preserving its partial-codex and copyright status.

open_questions.question: What explicit editorial policy governed Wright's expansions, punctuation, letterforms, word division, and treatment of manuscript gaps?
next_steps: Compare Wright line by line with direct f.52v photography or Ker's facsimile.
Consult any journal editorial correspondence or later assessments in Clarke.
Treat Wright's visible paragraphing and notes as edition features until manuscript correspondence is verified.

open_questions.question: Does the CME root Unlicense intentionally apply to XML markup and data in CME00137?
next_steps: Request a path-specific data/XML rights clarification from the repository maintainer.
Do not broaden software-worded root license text to upstream editions, U-M encoding, catalog records, scans, or manuscript images.
